Technical Guide

Fabric GSM Guide: Everything a Clothing Brand Needs to Know

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 10 min read
Home / Blog / Fabric GSM Guide: Everything a Clothing Brand Needs to Know

GSM (grams per square meter) is one of the most important specifications in clothing manufacturing. It determines a garment's weight, feel, durability, and perceived quality. Choosing the right GSM for your product category and brand positioning can mean the difference between a premium piece and a cheap throwaway. At FABRIOZA, we help brands select the perfect fabric weight for every product in their collection.

What Does GSM Mean?

GSM measures fabric weight — specifically, how many grams one square meter of fabric weighs. Higher GSM = heavier, thicker, more durable. Lower GSM = lighter, thinner, more breathable. GSM measures weight, not quality — a 180gsm premium cotton can feel better than a 220gsm cheap cotton.

GSM Guide for T-Shirts

GSMWeightBest ForRetail Price Position
120-140LightweightUndershirts, summer basicsBudget
150-170StandardRetail basics, promotionalMid-range
180-200PremiumBrand standard, soft handPremium
220-300HeavyweightStreetwear, vintage stylePremium+

GSM Guide for Hoodies and Sweatshirts

GSMWeightBest ForSeason
250-280LightweightLayering, gym, warm climatesSpring/Summer
300-320Mid-weightAll-purpose, retail standardAll seasons
340-380HeavyweightPremium streetwear, warmthFall/Winter
400+Ultra-heavyweightLuxury, statement piecesWinter

GSM Guide for Sportswear/Activewear

GSMCompressionBest For
150-180LightYoga, loungewear
180-220MediumGym, general training
220-280HighRunning, HIIT, compression

GSM Guide for Tracksuits

GSMStyleBest For
240-280Lightweight tracksuitWarm-up, travel
280-320Standard tracksuitEveryday, retail
320-380Premium tracksuitStreetwear, luxury

How GSM Affects Brand Positioning

Heavyweight GSM (320+) signals quality and commands higher retail prices. Customers associate weight with value — a 400gsm hoodie feels premium in the hand and justifies a $80-120 retail price. Budget brands use 150-180gsm to hit lower price points.

How to Choose the Right GSM

Consider your target retail price, brand positioning, product category, and season. For a premium streetwear brand: 340-380gsm hoodies, 220-260gsm tees. For a budget-friendly gym brand: 280-320gsm hoodies, 160-180gsm performance tees.

GSM Options at FABRIOZA

We offer every GSM weight from 120 to 400+ across cotton, polyester, spandex, and blend fabrics. Our team recommends the ideal weight based on your product type and brand positioning — at no charge.

FABRIOZA's team will recommend the exact weight for your product. Free consultation.

Frequently Asked Questions

What GSM is best for a premium hoodie brand?

340-380 GSM is the sweet spot for premium streetwear. It signals quality without being uncomfortably heavy.

Does higher GSM always mean better quality?

No. GSM measures weight, not quality. A 180gsm premium ringspun cotton can outperform a 220gsm basic cotton in softness and durability.

What GSM should I choose for summer t-shirts?

150-180 GSM for standard retail. 120-140 GSM for lightweight summer-specific styles.

Can I get different GSMs for the same design?

Yes, but each GSM requires separate production setup. We recommend starting with one weight and expanding based on demand.

How do I specify GSM in my order?

Tell us your target GSM or describe the feel you want ("premium heavyweight like Fear of God"). We'll match it.
